scoreableRoutes.read: Shot-opportunity probe found scoreable targets in 61 sampled windows; 82% had a lane with 2+ targets, lane diversity was 1, and center-lane bias was 0.11.

stage7LanguageWeaknessesFound: Stage 7 RED fields were too numeric to express bee-line vs butterfly-line teaching roles without extension fields.
Stage 7 lowerFieldShare was too blunt for Stage 3 because upper-band scoreability and lower-field occupancy can conflict.
Stage 7 path-family authority fields needed a clearer target-conformance vs source-promotion split.
Stage 7 provenance was group-level; Stage 3 needs field-level provenance for entry side, score window, exit gesture, and inferred roles.
languageAdequacy.recommendationRead: The RED can guide a non-overwriting Stage 3 candidate-trial gate if the gate keeps semantic provenance and does not treat numeric lower-field/object-track values as sole truth.

runtimePromotionBlockers: Stage 3 direct target-video object-track fit remains below the first runtime promotion floor.
Group 1: runtime entry side does not match human contract entry side; runtime exit side does not match human contract exit side.

summary.read: The RED can guide a non-overwriting Stage 3 candidate-trial gate if the gate keeps semantic provenance and does not treat numeric lower-field/object-track values as sole truth.

nextBestStep: Build a Stage 3 non-overwriting candidate-trial gate that evaluates semantic line-role, upper-band score-window, path-family, object-track, spacing/readability, scoreable-route, and no-combat guardrails before any runtime source edit.
